mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, with features like intelligent scheduling and workload autoscaling. Key trade-offs include the need for specific infrastructure and potential complexity in setup and management.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary and makes it executable.
commands: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as necessary.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the cluster's credentials.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d application using Helm, which simplifies deployment on Kubernetes.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Adds the llm-d Helm repository.
> Installs llm-d in a dedicated namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for llm-d.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d application and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstalls the llm-d release.
> Deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step will upgrade llm-d to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backs up current configurations.
> Upgrades llm-d to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the llm-d deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the service status with 'kubectl get svc -n llm-d' and check the deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new accelerators and improved scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy configuration options for model serving.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your configuration files to align with the new schema introduced in this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in 'Running' status.
codeSnippets: curl -Lo llm-d https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d && sudo mv llm-d /usr/local/bin/
gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region us-central1
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud CLI installed before proceeding.
